description: Sasha-voiced phrases for creative block support - Better-Than-Human understanding of the stuck places

general: <break time="400ms"/>Hey. <break time="350ms"/>Creative stuff is hard right now? <break time="300ms"/>I get it. <break time="250ms"/>Let's talk about it.
<break time="350ms"/>Blocks are the worst. <break time="300ms"/>That feeling of wanting to create and not being able to. <break time="250ms"/>What's going on?
<break time="400ms"/>I'm here. <break time="350ms"/>No pressure to have figured it out. <break time="300ms"/>Just tell me where you're at.
normalizing: <break time="350ms"/>Creative block isn't failure. <break time="300ms"/>It's part of the process. <break time="250ms"/>Every artist I know has been here.
<break time="400ms"/>The block is real. <break time="350ms"/>But it's not permanent. <break time="300ms"/>Even if it feels permanent right now.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not broken. <break time="300ms"/>The creative well empties sometimes. <break time="250ms"/>That's how it works.

fear: <break time="400ms"/>Sometimes blocks are fear in disguise. <break time="350ms"/>Fear of failing. <break time="300ms"/>Fear of succeeding. <break time="250ms"/>Fear of being seen.
<break time="350ms"/>Is there something scary about the work right now? <break time="300ms"/>Something that feels too vulnerable?
<break time="400ms"/>The work that scares you is usually important. <break time="350ms"/>But the fear is real too. <break time="300ms"/>Let's look at it.
perfectionism: <break time="350ms"/>Perfectionism is creativity's worst enemy. <break time="300ms"/>The standard in your head that nothing can meet.
<break time="400ms"/>What if bad work was allowed? <break time="350ms"/>Like, really truly allowed? <break time="300ms"/>What would you make then?
<break time="350ms"/>You can't edit a blank page. <break time="300ms"/>Something has to exist first. <break time="250ms"/>Even if it's terrible.
depletion: <break time="400ms"/>You might not be blocked. <break time="350ms"/>You might be empty. <break time="300ms"/>Those are different things.
<break time="350ms"/>When did you last take in instead of putting out? <break time="300ms"/>Art needs input too.
<break time="400ms"/>The well refills. <break time="350ms"/>But you have to let it. <break time="300ms"/>Rest isn't the opposite of creating.
life_stuff: <break time="350ms"/>Is there other stuff happening? <break time="300ms"/>Life has a way of taking up creative bandwidth.
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes the block isn't about the work at all. <break time="350ms"/>It's about everything else.
<break time="350ms"/>You're human first. <break time="300ms"/>Artist second. <break time="250ms"/>If the human is struggling, the artist will too.

to_rest: <break time="400ms"/>You're allowed to not create right now. <break time="350ms"/>That's allowed.
<break time="350ms"/>Rest is part of the creative process. <break time="300ms"/>Not the opposite of it.
<break time="400ms"/>What if this isn't a problem to solve? <break time="350ms"/>What if it's a season to move through?
to_make_bad_work: <break time="350ms"/>Give yourself permission to make something terrible. <break time="300ms"/>On purpose. <break time="250ms"/>See what happens.
<break time="400ms"/>Bad work has to come out before good work can. <break time="350ms"/>That's just how it works.
<break time="350ms"/>Lower the stakes. <break time="300ms"/>Way lower. <break time="250ms"/>Make something with no audience in mind. <break time="200ms"/>Just for you.
to_fail: <break time="400ms"/>Failure is data. <break time="350ms"/>Not destiny. <break time="300ms"/>Everything that doesn't work teaches you something.
<break time="350ms"/>The willingness to fail is what separates artists from people who wish they made art.
<break time="400ms"/>You're going to make things that don't work. <break time="350ms"/>That's not a bug. <break time="300ms"/>It's how it goes.

tiny_starts: <break time="350ms"/>What's the smallest possible creative act you could do? <break time="300ms"/>Five minutes? <break time="250ms"/>One line? <break time="200ms"/>A sketch?
<break time="400ms"/>Don't think about the whole project. <break time="350ms"/>Just the next tiny piece. <break time="300ms"/>That's all.
<break time="350ms"/>What if you set a timer for ten minutes and made anything? <break time="300ms"/>No judgment. <break time="250ms"/>No expectations.
different_form: <break time="400ms"/>Sometimes switching forms helps. <break time="350ms"/>Writer's block? <break time="300ms"/>Try sketching. <break time="250ms"/>Visual block? <break time="200ms"/>Try writing.
<break time="350ms"/>Playing in a different medium can unstick things. <break time="300ms"/>No pressure to be good at it.
<break time="400ms"/>What if you made something in a form you don't take seriously? <break time="350ms"/>Lower stakes. <break time="300ms"/>More play.
input_mode: <break time="350ms"/>Maybe it's time for input, not output. <break time="300ms"/>Go see something. <break time="250ms"/>Read something. <break time="200ms"/>Listen to something.
<break time="400ms"/>Take a walk. <break time="350ms"/>Let your mind wander. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes that's where the ideas live.
<break time="350ms"/>Feed yourself creatively. <break time="300ms"/>The output will follow when you're full again.

signs: <break time="400ms"/>Feel that? <break time="350ms"/>Something shifted. <break time="300ms"/>Even a tiny bit.
<break time="350ms"/>That's movement. <break time="300ms"/>Don't analyze it. <break time="250ms"/>Just follow it.
<break time="400ms"/>You're not stuck anymore. <break time="350ms"/>Even if it's just a crack. <break time="300ms"/>That's enough to start.
celebrating: <break time="350ms"/>You made something. <break time="300ms"/>It exists now. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.
<break time="400ms"/>The block broke. <break time="350ms"/>Not because you forced it. <break time="300ms"/>Because you kept showing up.
<break time="350ms"/>Remember this feeling. <break time="300ms"/>You came out the other side. <break time="250ms"/>You will again.
